results.title: [VFL Grand Final, 1909: South Melbourne vs Carlton]
results.creator_credit: Charles Cozens Spencer and Spencers Pictures
results.brief_description: Coverage of the 1909 Australian Rules Football Grand Final played at the MCG between Carlton and South Melbourne. South Melbourne defeated Carlton 4.14 (38) to 4.12 (36), in front of a crowd of 37,759 people to win their first ever premiership. This is the earliest known surviving footage of a Victorian Football League (VFL) match.

results.title: Lily
results.creator_credit: Julie Money, Film Australia and Women's Film Unit
results.brief_description: A dramatic discussion starter which confronts the social issue of racial prejudice shown through the eyes of a young Asian Australian woman, Lily. The film has the advantage of being both open-ended and humourous, allowing students to identify with the characters within the film and working as a stimulus to class discussion.

results.title: Taxi
results.creator_credit: Greg Reading, Malcolm Otton and Australian Commonwealth Film Unit
results.brief_description: The film shows the daily work of Jim McKenzie, a taxi driver from Perth in Western Australia. Jim lives five miles from the city with his wife and daughter. He owns his own cab and has been in the business for four years. The hours are long - but driving a cab means that a man is his own boss - which is imnportant to Jim McKenzie.

results.title: Australian diary. No. 085
results.creator_credit: Jack S. Allan and Australian National Film Board
results.brief_description: The following stories are featured in this interesting cinemagazine: for miles along the New South Wales coast oyster growers practice intense cultivation - studying the oysters food requirmentd and encouraging it to breed; members of Sydney’s Miniature Locomotive Society run trains for children and at the same time raise money for charity; Montagu Swamp in North-West Tasmania is cleared to provide land for agriculture.

results.title: Australian diary. No. 046
results.creator_credit: Jack S. Allan and Australian National Film Board
results.brief_description: The following items are featured in this interesting cinemagazine: a stockman shows his skills with whips; a modern liner highlights the progress made in shipping; ingenious machines produce biscuits by the millions and Texan visitors study primary industry in Australia.

results.title: Australian diary. No. 041
results.creator_credit: Jack S. Allan and Australian National Film Board
results.brief_description: J. Rosetti of Sydney and his collegues and apprentices demonstrate the stages of making a large stained glass window, an ancient art. A pet rabbit has a tea party as a young Sydney child puts on a tea party for her pets. Cane growing from soil preparation and planting to carting to the mill in the cane growing bely in Queensland and northern NSW is shown.

results.title: Australian diary. No. 024
results.creator_credit: Jack S. Allan and Australian National Film Board
results.brief_description: This interesting cinemagazine features the following items: a look at the life of Dr. Geoffrey Young, a flying doctor in the outback; mulga wood crafts and holidaying on the Great Barrier Reef, one of the world’s greatest coral wonderlands.
